verdict_rationale: The plan's architectural premise is empirically sound: dispatch flip + SSRF bank patch + dedupe helper closes 4/4 RED tests. Verified via `tldr taint` on all 4 fixtures (cmd/path/deser show source+sink+flow tainted=true; ssrf shows source detected, sinks=[] due to bank gap on reqwest::blocking::get scoped_identifier). However the planning text contains TWO empirically wrong claims about line-scanner behavior that, while not GO-blockers, must be corrected before M2 launch: (1) plan §3 line 251 claims the line scanner emits CommandInjection on rust_command_injection_positive — empirically FALSE; the line `.arg("-c").arg(&cmd)` contains the substring `.arg("` which triggers the line-scanner's string-literal guard (vuln.rs:603-604), so HEAD line scanner emits ZERO findings on this fixture. The dedupe path is therefore a NO-OP on all 4 RED fixtures, not a 1-finding-collapse on the cmd_injection fixture. (2) The fixture paths contain `/tests/` so `is_rust_test_file` (vuln.rs:679-685) returns TRUE, suppressing all `.unwrap()` Panic emissions on the test fixtures — but on real-world Rust files (post-M2), every `.unwrap()` will emit. R2 in the plan (Panic flood) is correctly identified but understated: on the published binary running on user codebases, the elephant is real. Two new elephants surfaced: (E1) the pre-existing wildcard `("*", "get")` member_pattern in RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest (taint.rs:2467) is overly broad — once dispatch flips, ANY tainted `.get(...)` field-expression on a Rust file emits Ssrf (HashMap::get / Vec::get / Option::get on tainted data). At HEAD this wildcard is dead code (Rust files bypass canonical); post-M2 it becomes live and may produce SSRF false-positives in real Rust codebases. (E2) the line-scanner's CommandInjection guard relies on `!trimmed.contains(".arg(\"")` which is line-scoped substring; pathological constructed Rust files with both a string-literal `.arg("...")` AND a real `.arg(&tainted)` on different lines will still emit only canonical CommandInjection — but that's the CORRECT behavior post-dedupe. The risk is LOW for the 4 carry-forward tests but MEDIUM for real-world UX.

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: Dispatch shape at vuln.rs:368-370 is the single early-return for .rs
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Read vuln.rs:354-419 directly. Lines 368-370 contain `if matches!(path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()), Some("rs")) { return Ok(analyze_rust_file(path, &source)); }`. analyze_file is the SOLE caller of both analyze_rust_file (via early return) and scan_vulnerabilities (via fall-through match at L373). Single dispatch site. No additional .rs routing in scan_vulnerabilities or upstream.

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: analyze_rust_file emits 5 distinct VulnType variants across 7 trigger patterns
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Read vuln.rs:449-630 fully. Triggers verified at line numbers: UnsafeCode L464-483 (unsafe { without SAFETY:); MemorySafety L485-503 (transmute); MemorySafety L505-527 (std::ptr::* / core::ptr::* / ptr::read/write); Panic L529-546 (.unwrap() guarded by !is_rust_test_file); SqlInjection L548-569 (format! + SQL keyword + {}/{/+); MemorySafety L571-595 (from_utf8_unchecked / .as_bytes()[ / .as_bytes().get_unchecked); CommandInjection L597-622 (Command::new( + subsequent .arg(non-string-literal) within block). 5 VulnType variants confirmed.

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns at taint.rs:2464-2477 lacks reqwest::blocking::get
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Read taint.rs:2464-2477 directly. Entries: ("*", "get"), ("*", "post"), ("", "reqwest::get"), ("", "reqwest::Client"), ("", "ureq::get"), ("", "ureq::post"), ("", "hyper::Client"), ("", "Url::parse"). NO reqwest::blocking::* entry. Fixture rust/ssrf_positive.rs line 3 calls `reqwest::blocking::get(&u).unwrap()`. The scoped_identifier text is `reqwest::blocking::get` which does NOT match any current member_pattern (the unbounded `("*", "get")` wildcard is for field_expression receiver-method splits, not scoped_identifier path text).

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: Canonical pipeline detects source/sink/flow on cmd/path/deser fixtures at HEAD
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Ran `./target/release/tldr taint` on all 4 RED fixtures. Empirical results: command_injection_positive.rs → sink_type=shell_exec, tainted=true, flow constructed; path_traversal_positive.rs → sink_type=file_open, tainted=true; deserialization_positive.rs → sink_type=deserialize, tainted=true; ssrf_positive.rs → source detected, sinks=[], flows=[] (BANK GAP CONFIRMED).

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: tldr vuln returns 0 findings on rust_command_injection_positive (test is RED at HEAD)
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Ran `./target/release/tldr vuln crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/rust/command_injection_positive.rs --lang rust --format json --quiet`. Output: `{"findings": [], "summary": {"total_findings": 0, ...}}`. Confirms test RED at HEAD.

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: tldr vuln returns 0 findings on rust_command_injection_string_literal_fp (FP-clean baseline at HEAD)
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Ran `./target/release/tldr vuln crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/rust/command_injection_string_literal_fp.rs --lang rust --format json --quiet`. Output: 0 findings. The format!() line lacks SQL keywords (FORMAT/DOC/MORE upper-case have no SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/FROM/WHERE substring), no Command::new outside string literals, no .unwrap() in this fn body. Both layers emit zero.

Read types.rs:1361-1372 — `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] pub enum VulnType` with variants SqlInjection, CommandInjection, UnsafeCode, MemorySafety, Panic. Empirical confirmation: ran tldr vuln on a real Rust file (vuln.rs itself) — output emits `"vuln_type": "unsafe_code"`, `"vuln_type": "memory_safety"`, etc. Schema OK; tests assert findings_of_type(report, "command_injection") which matches snake_case wire format.

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: Plan §3 line 251 claim that line scanner emits CommandInjection on cmd_injection_positive fixture is WRONG
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Trace through analyze_rust_file on the fixture line 3 (`std::process::Command::new("sh").arg("-c").arg(&cmd).status().unwrap();`): trimmed.contains("Command::new(") → TRUE, in_command_block=true. Then trimmed.contains(".arg(") → TRUE BUT trimmed.contains(".arg(\"") → ALSO TRUE because `.arg("-c")` is on the same line. Guard fires (vuln.rs:603-604), CommandInjection NOT emitted. Also `.unwrap()` on this line: is_rust_test_file=TRUE because path contains `/tests/`, so Panic SUPPRESSED. Net line-scanner output for this fixture at HEAD: ZERO findings. The `tldr vuln` empirical test (returns 0 findings) corroborates this. The plan's claim that dedupe collapses BOTH layers' CommandInjection on this fixture is therefore wrong — the line scanner emits 0; dedupe is a no-op for ALL 4 RED fixtures.

verified_at_head_7033d30.claim: is_rust_test_file at vuln.rs:679-685 returns TRUE for fixture paths containing `/tests/`
verified_at_head_7033d30.verification: Read vuln.rs:679-685: `path_str.contains("/tests/") || path_str.contains("\\tests\\") || path_str.ends_with("_test.rs") || path_str.ends_with("tests.rs")`. Fixture path is `crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/rust/command_injection_positive.rs` — contains `/tests/`. Returns TRUE → all `.unwrap()` Panic findings suppressed for the fixtures. CRITICAL: Real-world Rust source paths (e.g. `src/lib.rs`, `crates/foo/src/main.rs`) do NOT contain `/tests/` → Panic emits per-unwrap.

tigers.scenario: Plan §3 line 251 claims `the 4 RED positive fixtures will fire CommandInjection from BOTH layers on the same line for command_injection_positive.rs; dedupe collapses them`. Empirically the line scanner fires ZERO findings on rust_command_injection_positive (string-literal guard `!trimmed.contains(".arg(\"")` matches the `.arg("-c")` substring on the same line). Dedupe is a no-op on ALL 4 RED fixtures. This means M3's `M3-dedupe-verification.json` cannot use any of the 4 RED fixtures as a dedupe demonstration — the constructed ad-hoc fixture is the ONLY way to verify dedupe behavior, and the plan §4 acknowledges this.

callers_traced.what_it_does_with_result: in_command_block=true on Command::new — but the very next conditional at L601-605 requires `.arg(` AND `!.arg(\"` AND `!.arg(\'`. The fixture's all-in-one-line `.arg("-c").arg(&cmd)` line fails the !.arg(\" guard and emits NOTHING.

Confirmed empirically by running tldr vuln on the fixture: 0 findings at HEAD. If the line scanner had emitted CommandInjection, tldr vuln would already return 1 finding and the test would be GREEN — but it's RED.

tigers.discriminative_test: Construct a Rust fixture with line `std::process::Command::new("sh").arg(&cmd).status().unwrap();` (NO string-literal .arg between Command::new and the tainted .arg). Pre-M2: line scanner emits 1 CommandInjection (no string-literal guard fires). Post-M2: canonical also emits 1 CommandInjection on same line; dedupe collapses to 1. THIS is the dedupe demonstration; it's NOT command_injection_positive.rs.
tigers.mitigation: M3-dedupe-verification.json fixture must be constructed (NOT one of the 4 RED fixtures) to exercise the dedupe path. Document in plan-amendment that dedupe is a NO-OP for the 4 RED fixtures. Update plan §3 line 251 wording before M2 launch — replace `the 4 RED positive fixtures will fire CommandInjection from BOTH layers` with `none of the 4 RED positive fixtures exercises dual-emission; the line scanner emits 0 findings on cmd_injection_positive due to the string-literal guard at vuln.rs:603-604, and the other 3 fixtures lack any line-scanner trigger`. Dedupe correctness still requires M3 verification on a constructed fixture.

tigers.scenario: RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns at taint.rs:2467 contains `("*", "get")` — a wildcard receiver + method=`get`. At HEAD this entry is DEAD CODE for Rust because vuln.rs:368-370 routes .rs files away from canonical pipeline. Post-M2 dispatch flip, this entry becomes LIVE for every .rs file passing through canonical. The wildcard fires on field_expression nodes of shape `<receiver>.get(<args>)` — meaning HashMap::get, Vec::get, Option::get, BTreeMap::get, IndexMap::get, etc. on any TAINTED receiver/argument will emit Ssrf findings. Real-world Rust codebases use `.get()` extensively; post-M2 may produce a flood of Ssrf false positives. The 4 RED fixtures don't trigger this (none uses `.get(` field expression on tainted data), and the 4 string-literal-FP fixtures don't either, so M2 stop-thresholds will pass — but real-world UX may degrade.

callers_traced.what_it_does_with_result: ("*", "get") member_pattern in RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest. Used by the AST sink-matcher when iterating field_expression nodes. Wildcard receiver matches any object.

tigers.discriminative_test: Construct a Rust file: `pub fn handler() { let key = std::env::var("K").unwrap(); let map: std::collections::HashMap<String, i32> = ...; let _ = map.get(&key); }`. Pre-M2: tldr vuln returns 0 findings (dispatch bypass). Post-M2: tldr vuln returns 1 Ssrf finding (false positive — HashMap::get is not an HTTP request). If post-M2 returns 1 Ssrf finding for this fixture, the wildcard-get bank pattern is empirically problematic for real-world Rust UX.
tigers.mitigation: Out of M2 scope per plan §10 (M2 closes 4 RED tests; bank cleanup is a follow-on). M3 binary smoke MUST construct this discriminative fixture to QUANTIFY false-positive count post-M2 on representative HashMap::get / Vec::get / Option::get patterns. If the FP rate is high (e.g. >10% of synthetic Rust fixtures), surface as a follow-on milestone. Alternative: tighten `("*", "get")` to `("*", "get")` AND require receiver to be an http-client-like type — but that's a separate bank-cleanup milestone, NOT in M2 scope per atomic-commit boundary. Document this risk in M3-report.json explicitly.

tigers.scenario: Plan R2 acknowledges Panic flood from .unwrap() but understates the production impact. The 4 RED test fixtures lie under `/tests/` so `is_rust_test_file()` returns TRUE → no Panic emission. After M2 dispatch flip, the published binary running on a user's `crates/foo/src/main.rs` (no `/tests/` in path) will emit 1 Panic finding per `.unwrap()` — typical Rust production codebases have 10s-100s of `.unwrap()` calls. Combined with new canonical findings from dispatch flip, output for a real Rust file may grow 10-50x. Plan correctly notes pre-M2 line scanner already had this behavior on production code, but it ALSO claims this is `unchanged from HEAD; canonical findings are NET-NEW additions`. That's correct mechanically but misleading: the population of files that hit this code path expands post-M2 in practice (no dispatch issues, easier to reach). User UX on real codebases: high noise.

callers_traced.what_it_does_with_result: Emits Panic finding per .unwrap() in non-test code. After M2 dispatch flip, canonical findings ALSO emit on tainted .unwrap()-chains.

tigers.discriminative_test: Run post-M2 binary on a real-world Rust file (e.g. crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs ITSELF — not a test file). Count Panic findings. Quantify in M3-report.json. If count > 100 on ANY single workspace file, surface as `--no-smells` flag follow-on milestone.
tigers.mitigation: Pre-existing behavior at HEAD; M2 doesn't fundamentally change the noise floor — it adds canonical findings on top. Document in CHANGELOG entry §7. Future milestone may add `--no-smells` flag or move Panic to severity Info default-suppressed. NOT a M2 blocker. R2 in plan §9 is correct in spirit; the empirical magnitude should be quantified in M3 binary smoke.

tigers.scenario: Verify the 4 string-literal-FP fixtures do NOT exercise field_expression `.get()` calls. If any FP fixture mentions `.get()` (e.g. as a string-literal or comment that the canonical AST walker might tokenize), and a tainted source is in scope, the wildcard `("*", "get")` could fire and break the FP-clean baseline.

callers_traced.what_it_does_with_result: Read fixture: `format!("{} {}", doc, more)` — no `.get(` field expression in code, only string literal mentions. Tree-sitter doesn't tokenize string-literal contents as field expressions.

tigers.discriminative_test: M1 stop-threshold (the planning gate) explicitly captures `tldr vuln` output on all 4 FP fixtures pre-M2 (zero findings expected). M2 stop-threshold gates on the same 4 fixtures STAYING at zero. If post-M2 any FP fixture surfaces a wildcard-get Ssrf, the M2 commit gate fails — empirical safety net. Read all 4 FP fixtures: confirm none has `.get(` outside string literal.
tigers.mitigation: All 4 FP fixtures inspected: command_injection_string_literal_fp.rs has only `format!("{} {}", doc, more)` — no `.get(` field expression. Plan likely safe. M1 baseline + M2 stop-threshold provide empirical guardrail. If a future bank addition introduces an FP, it's caught by these tests.

tigers.scenario: Plan §5 dedupe predicate uses `c.line == line_f.line`. Need to confirm that BOTH layers emit findings with line numbers from the SAME 1-indexed convention. If the canonical pipeline reports line=3 (sink line in fixture) and the line scanner reports line=command_block_start_line (line 3 also for cmd_injection_positive — Command::new and .arg(&cmd) on the same line, so command_block_start_line=3 and emission line=max(3,3)=3) — matches. But line-scanner CommandInjection emission uses `command_block_start_line.max(line_number)` (vuln.rs:616) where command_block_start_line tracks the LINE THAT HAD Command::new — but if Command::new and .arg(&cmd) are on DIFFERENT lines, command_block_start_line could be earlier than the .arg line. The canonical pipeline likely reports the .arg(&cmd) line (the actual sink invocation). Edge case: Command::new on line 5, .arg(&cmd) on line 7 — line scanner emits at max(5,7)=7, canonical emits at 7 → dedupe matches. But if the sink line in canonical is the call_expression line (5, where Command::new is) instead of the field_expression line (7), they differ → dedupe fails to collapse → 2 findings.

callers_traced.what_it_does_with_result: Line scanner emits at `command_block_start_line.max(line_number)` — picks LATER of (Command::new line, .arg line).
scan_vulnerabilities returns Finding with sink.line. The canonical pipeline's sink line for Command::new(...).arg(&cmd) chain — need to verify whether it's the Command::new line or the .arg line.

tigers.discriminative_test: Construct a Rust fixture where `Command::new("sh")` is on line 5 and `.arg(&cmd)` is on line 7 (multi-line chain). Run tldr taint and tldr vuln post-M2. Compare line numbers. If line-scanner emits at line 7 and canonical at line 5, dedupe FAILS to collapse → 2 findings. If both at line 7, dedupe collapses correctly. Document in M3-dedupe-verification.json.
tigers.mitigation: M3-dedupe-verification.json MUST include a multi-line Command::new + .arg chain as one of the test cases. If line-key mismatch surfaces, the dedupe predicate must be widened — e.g. (vuln_type, ±1 line tolerance) or (vuln_type, expression-text-substring). The 4 RED fixtures all use single-line Command::new(...) on line 3, so the bug is INVISIBLE on those — M2 stop-threshold will GREEN even if dedupe is buggy in this corner case.

elephants.scenario: The pre-existing wildcard `("*", "get")` at taint.rs:2467 has been DEAD CODE for .rs files since the dispatch bypass was introduced in vuln-migration-v1 M4. Nobody has ever observed its behavior on real Rust files. Post-M2 it suddenly becomes LIVE on all .rs files. This is one of those `it never worked because we never tested it` cases. The bank entry was added (commit history check: VULN-MIGRATION-V1 M2 per the comment at taint.rs:2459-2463) WITH THE INTENT of being live, but the dispatch bypass made it dormant. M2 inadvertently activates it without re-validating its correctness. Same applies to ("*", "post") at line 2468.
elephants.implication: M2 expands the live bank pattern surface area for Rust by ~8 patterns (the entire HttpRequest + FileOpen + Deserialize + ShellExec + CodeEval + FileWrite RUST_AST_SINKS arrays) without M1 having reviewed each pattern's FP risk. The plan correctly notes M1 captures FP-baseline pre-M2; M2 stop-threshold gates on `0 currently-GREEN tests transition RED`. But the test corpus may not exercise every newly-live bank pattern.
elephants.mitigation: M1 scope MUST include: enumerate every member_pattern in RUST_AST_SOURCES and RUST_AST_SINKS, and for each pattern that wasn't exercised by any GREEN test pre-M2, run a constructed mini-fixture through tldr vuln post-M2 to confirm no FP. This is a one-time validation; subsequent bank additions retain whatever empirical guardrails M3 establishes. Add to M1 scope: capture `tldr vuln` output on a curated set of synthetic Rust patterns (HashMap::get tainted, Vec::get tainted, file_path_join tainted, etc.) — quantify FP rate per bank pattern.

elephants.scenario: The plan's HEAD state claim of 158/166 GREEN on vuln_migration_v1_red is not re-verified at planning time (HEAD has advanced from 5d46628 to 7033d30 since the plan was written; subsequent commits added ruby-backtick-extraction-v1 and var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 plans but NO source code changes). The investigation.json at line 6 cites HEAD 5d46628; the plan was committed at 997557b. Drift is documentation-only between 997557b and 7033d30 — but if any subsequent commit slipped in a source change (e.g. an unrelated taint.rs touch), the 158/166 baseline could shift.
elephants.implication: If M1 captures a different baseline (e.g. 159/166 due to an untracked source change), M2 stop-threshold of 162/166 needs adjustment. Low risk because git log between 997557b and 7033d30 shows only continuum/ docs commits, but worth confirming.
elephants.mitigation: M1 first action: `git log --oneline 5d46628..HEAD -- crates/` to confirm zero source changes since planning baseline. If any surface, document and adjust M2 expected GREEN count.

elephants.scenario: Plan §11 validator_mandate `test_analyze_rust_unit_tests_preserved` claims `9 test_analyze_rust_*` unit tests at vuln.rs:925-1037 must remain GREEN. The investigation.json line 117-128 enumerates 7 distinct test names (test_collect_files_includes_rust + 6 test_analyze_rust_detects_*), with `supplementary` noting test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping and test_vuln_type_severity exist but are unrelated to analyze_rust_file. Plan §1 line 110 says `9 existing test_analyze_rust_*`. Reading vuln.rs:925-1037 directly: 7 #[test] fns named test_analyze_rust_* (test_analyze_rust_detects_unsafe_without_safety_comment, _command_and_sql_patterns, _transmute_usage, _raw_pointer_operation, _unwrap_in_non_test_code, _unchecked_bytes_patterns) + test_collect_files_includes_rust + test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping + test_vuln_type_severity = 9 #[test] fns total in this test module, of which only 6 actually exercise analyze_rust_file's body. Counting discrepancy is cosmetic but worth reconciling.
elephants.implication: M1 stop-threshold says `9/9 test_analyze_rust_* unit tests passing`. The actual `test_analyze_rust_*` (matched by name prefix) count is 6, not 9. M1 worker may report `6/6 GREEN` and confuse the orchestrator. If they run `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests` the count will be 9 #[test] fns total in the tests module.
elephants.mitigation: Plan-amendment: clarify M1/M2 stop-threshold to read `all 9 #[test] fns in commands::remaining::vuln::tests module` OR `all 6 test_analyze_rust_detects_* + test_collect_files_includes_rust = 7 unit tests directly testing the line scanner / collection`. Cosmetic only; doesn't block GO.

rationale_for_GO: Plan's central architectural premise is empirically verified: dispatch flip + SSRF bank patch closes 4/4 RED tests. tldr taint shows source/sink/flow tainted=true on 3/4 fixtures at HEAD; ssrf bank gap is the SOLE blocker for the 4th and is addressed atomically.
The wire format (snake_case) and VulnFinding schema are unchanged — public-API contract preserved.
The exhaustive map_core_vuln_type match (vuln.rs:437-447, NO _ arm) prevents silent VulnType mislabeling — closing-#11 protection retained.
M2 atomic-commit boundary correctly identified: dispatch flip + dedupe + SSRF bank + doc-comment update MUST ship together. Splitting any pair creates intermediate-state regressions per plan §8.
Test corpus structure (4 RED + 4 string-literal-FP regression-guards) provides bidirectional gates: positive tests close, FP tests stay clean.
Existing 9 #[test] fns in commands::remaining::vuln::tests module are decoupled from analyze_file dispatch (they call analyze_rust_file directly) — M2 dispatch-only changes cannot break them.
Rollback path is single-step: `git revert <M2_sha>` reverts atomically; no orphan partial states.

amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.current_text: the 4 RED positive fixtures will fire CommandInjection from BOTH layers on the same line for command_injection_positive.rs; dedupe collapses them
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.corrected_text: None of the 4 RED positive fixtures exercises dual-emission. The line scanner emits 0 findings on cmd_injection_positive due to the string-literal guard at vuln.rs:603-604 (`.arg("-c")` on the same line as `.arg(&cmd)` triggers `!trimmed.contains(".arg(\"")` → false → guard fires → no emission). The other 3 fixtures (path_traversal, ssrf, deserialization) lack any line-scanner trigger entirely. Dedupe is therefore a NO-OP on all 4 RED fixtures. Dedupe correctness MUST be verified via the M3 ad-hoc constructed fixture (single-line Command::new + .arg(&cmd) without preceding .arg(\"...\")), NOT against any of the 4 RED fixtures.
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.rationale: M3-dedupe-verification.json is the canonical demonstration; plan §4 already correctly anticipates this. Wording fix is for traceability.

amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.current_text: Run a representative non-Rust file (python/sql_injection_positive.py) through the binary to verify non-Rust dispatch is unchanged.
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.corrected_text: ADD to M3 scope: construct 3-5 synthetic Rust fixtures exercising newly-live bank patterns post-M2: (a) HashMap::get on tainted key, (b) Vec::get on tainted index, (c) Option::get with tainted, (d) handler with reqwest::blocking::get tainted (the SSRF closure path), (e) std::fs::read_to_string on tainted (FileOpen path). Quantify FP count per pattern. If `("*", "get")` wildcard surfaces FP rate >10% on synthetic patterns, surface as a bank-tightening follow-on milestone.
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.rationale: Catches the wildcard-get bank pattern issue (T2/E1) before it ships in a published binary. Constructed fixtures are cheap; results are diagnostic.

amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.current_text: Construct an ad-hoc dedupe-verification fixture (NOT added to the test corpus) with both canonical CommandInjection trigger and line-scanner CommandInjection trigger on the same line
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.corrected_text: Construct THREE ad-hoc fixtures: (1) single-line same-vuln-type overlap (must collapse to 1 finding); (2) MULTI-LINE Command::new + .arg(&cmd) chain where Command::new is on line N and .arg is on line N+2 (verify dedupe-key precision per T5); (3) line-scanner UnsafeCode on line K + canonical CommandInjection on line K (different VulnTypes; must emit 2 findings).
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.rationale: Catches dedupe predicate brittleness on multi-line chains (T5). Cheap to add.

amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.current_text: All 9 test_analyze_rust_* unit tests must remain GREEN
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.corrected_text: All 9 #[test] fns in `commands::remaining::vuln::tests` module must remain GREEN. The 9 fns are: test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping, test_vuln_type_severity, test_collect_files_includes_rust, test_analyze_rust_detects_unsafe_without_safety_comment, test_analyze_rust_detects_command_and_sql_patterns, test_analyze_rust_detects_transmute_usage, test_analyze_rust_detects_raw_pointer_operation, test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code, test_analyze_rust_detects_unchecked_bytes_patterns. Of these, 6 prefix-match `test_analyze_rust_detects_*`. Cosmetic clarification.
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.rationale: Avoids confusion when M1 worker counts test names by prefix.

amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.current_text: (implicit assumption that HEAD baseline matches plan's 158/166 claim)
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.corrected_text: M1 first action: `git log --oneline 5d46628..HEAD -- crates/` to confirm zero source-code changes between planning baseline and M1 launch. If any source change exists, document the diff in M1-report.json and adjust M2 expected GREEN count accordingly.
amendments_required_pre_m2_launch.rationale: Detects undocumented baseline drift before M2 commits to a 162/166 target.

summary: Plan's architectural premise verified empirically: dispatch flip + SSRF bank patch + dedupe helper closes 4/4 RED carry-forward tests.
